events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":70,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.85,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Exceeded sidewall pressure/jamming during wire pulling indicates conductors may have been damaged by excessive pulling tension or sidewall bearing pressure at bends","Conductor identification per NEC 200.6 and 210.5 must be verified—damaged insulation may compromise identification markings","Feeder and branch circuit conductors pulled under jamming conditions may have insulation damage not visible at terminations"],"actions":["Inspect all conductors pulled through affected raceway bends for insulation damage and sidewall pressure effects","Verify pulling calculations did not exceed maximum sidewall bearing pressure limits for the conduit/raceway and bend radius","Perform megger/insulation resistance testing on affected feeders and branch circuits before energization","Re-pull damaged conductors where insulation integrity is compromised","Verify conductor identification compliance with NEC 200.6 (grounded conductor identification) and 210.5 (branch circuit identification)"],"rationale":"Exceeded sidewall pressure/jamming during wire pulling is a planning/execution defect that can damage conductor insulation and compromise circuit integrity. At rough-complete stage with conductors installed, this is non-compliant and must be corrected before closeout. NEC 200.6 and 210.5 identification requirements must also be verified given potential marking damage from jamming.","references":["NEC 200.6","NEC 210.5"]}
